title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new brand of subscription-based meal kits.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve been trying 'Chef-Box' this month. It’s certainly saved me a lot of time on grocery shopping. || Woman: [1]I found the amount of plastic packaging in each box to be quite shocking[/1], to be honest. It felt like everything was individually wrapped in film. || Man: I agree. It’s a bit hard to square with their 'eco-friendly' branding. || Woman: [2]What I really liked, though, was the variety of the recipes[/2]. I would never have thought of making a Moroccan tagine on a Tuesday night. || Man: I agree. It’s definitely expanded my cooking repertoire. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great way to try new things, I just wish they’d figure out a more sustainable way to ship the ingredients.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve been trying 'Chef-Box' this month. It’s certainly saved me a lot of time on grocery shopping.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the amount of plastic packaging in each box to be quite shocking, to be honest. It felt like everything was individually wrapped in film.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a bit hard to square with their 'eco-friendly' branding.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really liked, though, was the variety of the recipes. I would never have thought of making a Moroccan tagine on a Tuesday night.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s definitely expanded my cooking repertoire.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great way to try new things, I just wish they’d figure out a more sustainable way to ship the ingredients.

questions.question: The woman is critical of the service because of

options.A: the high cost of the weekly subscription.
options.B: the excessive use of non-recyclable materials.
options.C: the limited time given to prepare the meals.

questions.explanation: She mentions being 'shocked' by the amount of plastic packaging used [1].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the meal kits have
questions.explanation: They both highlight the 'variety of recipes' and that it 'expanded their cooking repertoire' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a professional development workshop on 'Active Listening'.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That workshop was a bit more challenging than I expected. I never realized how much I interrupt people! || Woman: [3]I suspect the trainer was a bit too focused on the theory rather than giving us practical tools for the office[/3]. || Man: It was a bit 'academic', I suppose. But the roleplay was useful. || Woman: [4]I was disappointed that we didn't get a chance to discuss how to handle difficult clients specifically[/4]. That was what I really needed. || Man: I agree. It was all a bit too general to be immediately useful. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a fine skill to have, but I’m not sure I’ve come away with anything I can actually use in my team meeting tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That workshop was a bit more challenging than I expected. I never realized how much I interrupt people!

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the trainer was a bit too focused on the theory rather than giving us practical tools for the office.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It was a bit 'academic', I suppose. But the roleplay was useful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I was disappointed that we didn't get a chance to discuss how to handle difficult clients specifically. That was what I really needed.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It was all a bit too general to be immediately useful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a fine skill to have, but I’m not sure I’ve come away with anything I can actually use in my team meeting tomorrow.

questions.question: The woman suggests that the workshop was

options.A: too basic for her level of experience.

questions.explanation: She thinks it was too 'focused on theory' rather than 'practical tools for the office' [3].

questions.question: Both speakers agree that the session was lacking in
questions.explanation: They both feel it was 'too general' and missed 'specific challenges' like handling difficult clients [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new local bakery specializing in sourdough.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you tried the olive loaf from 'The Rise'? It’s absolutely delicious. || Woman: [5]I found the staff to be a bit dismissive when I asked about gluten-free options[/5], to be honest. They weren't very helpful at all. || Man: I see what you mean. They are a bit 'purist' about their bread. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the open-plan kitchen, though[/6]. It was fascinating to watch them kneading the dough while you wait. || Man: I agree. It adds a real sense of authenticity to the place. || Woman: Definitely. I just wish the service was as warm as the ovens.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you tried the olive loaf from 'The Rise'? It’s absolutely delicious.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the staff to be a bit dismissive when I asked about gluten-free options, to be honest. They weren't very helpful at all.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. They are a bit 'purist' about their bread.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the open-plan kitchen, though. It was fascinating to watch them kneading the dough while you wait.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It adds a real sense of authenticity to the place.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. I just wish the service was as warm as the ovens.

questions.question: What did the woman dislike about her visit to the bakery?

options.A: the quality of the baked goods
options.B: the unhelpful attitude of the employees
options.C: the high price of the specialty loaves

questions.explanation: She describes the staff as 'dismissive' and 'not very helpful' when she had a question [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the bakery's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'open-plan kitchen' where you can watch the bread being made [6].
